2606.13452.title: Examining the Cognitive Gap Between Authors and Peer Reviewers on Academic Paper Novelty
2606.13452.paper_summary: Novelty is a crucial metric for assessing the quality of academic papers. Scholars strive to highlight the novel aspects of their work, particularly in the title, abstract, and introduction. Peer review, serving as the gatekeeper of scientific rigor, rigorously evaluates the novelty of papers, yet a cognitive gap may exist between author self-promotion and reviewer evaluation. To investigate this, we analyzed 15,328 academic papers published in Nature Communications from 2016 to 2021, along with their peer-review comments. We found that both reviewers and authors emphasize result-oriented innovation, with reviewers adopting a more comprehensive evaluation perspective. Furthermore, by examining promotional intensity against inherent paper novelty, we found that its effect depends on the paper's actual innovation level. Highly innovative papers benefit from stronger promotional language, receiving more positive evaluations. We also found that promotional language significantly correlates with reviewer disagreement on novelty specifically for papers of moderate innovativeness, whereas it has negligible impact for papers with either very high or very low novelty. This reveals how promotional language operates most prominently in the gray area of academic evaluation.
2606.13452.paper_summary_zh: 新穎性是評估學術論文質量的關鍵指標。學者們努力在其作品中突出新穎的方面，特別是在標題、摘要和引言中。同行評審作為科學嚴謹性的守門人，嚴格評估論文的新穎性，但作者自我推廣與評審評價之間可能存在認知差距。為了調查這一點，我們分析了2016年至2021年間發表在《自然通訊》上的15,328篇學術論文及其同行評審意見。我們發現，評審和作者都強調以結果為導向的創新，評審則採取更全面的評估視角。此外，通過檢查推廣強度與論文內在新穎性之間的關係，我們發現其影響取決於論文的實際創新水平。高度創新的論文受益於更強的推廣語言，獲得更積極的評價。我們還發現，推廣語言與評審在新穎性上的分歧顯著相關，特別是對於中等創新性的論文，而對於新穎性非常高或非常低的論文則影響微乎其微。這揭示了推廣語言在學術評估灰色地帶的運作方式。
2606.13452.author: Chenggang Yang et.al.
